 {"id":"bd-1jt","title":"EPIC: Fallback Release Infrastructure (ACFS)","description":"# Agent-Controlled Fallback System (ACFS) for Multi-Tool Releases

## Background & Problem Statement

GitHub Actions is limiting usage due to high volume (~$5k/month equivalent). When CI/CD jobs stay queued for >10 minutes, it indicates throttling. We need an automated fallback system that:

1. **Detects throttling** - Monitors GH Actions queue time, triggers fallback when jobs sit >10 min
2. **Builds locally** - Linux on Threadripper workstation (trj), macOS on Mac mini (mmini)
3. **Handles Windows** - Cross-compilation or other strategies TBD
4. **Uploads releases** - Manually create releases and upload binaries when throttled
5. **Smart installers** - curl|bash one-liners that try release first, build from source if stale

## Infrastructure Available

| Host | Alias | Purpose | Connection |
|------|-------|---------|------------|
| Threadripper Workstation | trj | Linux x86_64/ARM64 builds | local |
| Mac mini | mmini | macOS ARM64/x86_64 builds | ssh mmini |
| OVH servers | yto, fmd | Backup build capacity | ssh yto/fmd |

## Tools Affected (16 total from Dicklesworthstone toolchain)

cass, bv, cm, ubs, xf, ru, slb, caam, ntm, br, dcg, ms, wa, pt, rch, mcp_agent_mail

## Languages & Toolchains

- **Rust** (majority): cargo, rustup, cross-compilation via cargo-zigbuild or cross
- **Go**: go build, goreleaser  
- **TypeScript/Bun**: bun build, bun compile

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Any tool can be released within 30 minutes even when GH Actions is throttled
2. curl-bash installers work on Linux/macOS/Windows without manual intervention
3. Installers auto-detect stale releases (>10 commits behind) and offer source builds
4. Toolchain installation is automatic and non-destructive
5. All paths have unit tests and E2E validation scripts with detailed logging

## Key Design Decisions

1. **10-minute threshold**: Chosen as balance between patience and productivity
2. **Try release first**: Network download is faster than compilation for most users
3. **Commit-based staleness**: More accurate than date-based (active development = fresh releases)
4. **Non-destructive toolchains**: Never break existing installations  
5. **Detailed logging**: Every step logged for debugging failed installs

## Architecture Overview

\`\`\`
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    ACFS Release Pipeline                             │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  [1] Push tag v1.2.3                                                │
│         │                                                            │
│         ▼                                                            │
│  [2] GH Actions triggered                                           │
│         │                                                            │
│         ├──→ Queued <10min? ──→ Build & Release (normal path)       │
│         │                                                            │
│         └──→ Queued >10min? ──→ ACFS Fallback Triggered             │
│                    │                                                 │
│                    ▼                                                 │
│              [3] Local Builds                                       │
│                    │                                                 │
│         ┌─────────┴─────────┬────────────────┐                      │
│         ▼                   ▼                ▼                      │
│    Linux (trj)        macOS (mmini)    Windows (cross)              │
│         │                   │                │                      │
│         └─────────┬─────────┴────────────────┘                      │
│                   ▼                                                  │
│              [4] Upload binaries to GitHub Release                  │
│                   │                                                  │
│                   ▼                                                  │
│              [5] Update Homebrew/Scoop formulas                     │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                 Smart Curl-Bash Installer Flow                       │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  curl -sSL install.sh | bash                                        │
│         │                                                            │
│         ▼                                                            │
│  [1] Detect OS/Arch                                                 │
│         │                                                            │
│         ▼                                                            │
│  [2] Check latest release                                           │
│         │                                                            │
│         ├──→ Release exists & fresh (<10 commits behind)?           │
│         │         │                                                  │
│         │         └──→ Download binary ──→ Install ──→ Done         │
│         │                                                            │
│         └──→ Release missing or stale?                              │
│                   │                                                  │
│                   ▼                                                  │
│         [3] Prompt: "Build from source?"                            │
│                   │                                                  │
│                   ▼                                                  │
│         [4] Check/Install toolchain (Rust/Go/Bun)                   │
│                   │                                                  │
│                   ▼                                                  │
│         [5] Clone repo & build                                      │
│                   │                                                  │
│                   ▼                                                  │
│         [6] Install binary ──→ Done                                 │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
\`\`\`

## Dependency Graph (high-level themes)

1. **Theme 1: GH Actions Monitoring** - No deps, can start immediately
2. **Theme 2: Local Build Infrastructure** - Needs monitoring to know when to trigger
3. **Theme 3: Release Upload Automation** - Needs builds to have artifacts to upload
4. **Theme 4: Smart Installers** - Can be developed in parallel, integrates with releases
5. **Theme 5: Testing & Validation** - Depends on all above being implemented

## Success Criteria
- [ ] End-to-end fallback pipeline works for all tools
- [ ] Installers detect stale releases and offer source builds
- [ ] Cross-platform builds + releases verified
- [ ] Unit/integration/E2E tests pass with detailed logs

 {"id":"bd-1jt.1","title":"Theme 1: GH Actions Monitoring & Timeout Detection","description":"# Theme 1: GH Actions Monitoring & Timeout Detection

## Purpose
Detect when GH Actions runs are throttled (queued >10 minutes) and automatically trigger the fallback build system.

## Rationale
- GH Actions throttling manifests as prolonged queue time
- We need **deterministic** detection using structured API data (no human output parsing)
- 10-minute threshold balances patience with productivity

## Implementation: Use GitHub REST API JSON
Use the workflow runs endpoint and compute queue time from timestamps:
```bash
json=$(gh_api "repos/$owner/$repo/actions/runs?per_page=20")
queued=$(echo "$json" | jq -r '.workflow_runs[] | select(.status=="queued") | .created_at')
```

## Key Components
1. **dsr check** - Check if GH Actions is throttled for a repo
2. **dsr watch** - Continuously monitor and trigger fallback when needed
3. **dsr status** - Show current queue status across all repos

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Can detect a queued run within 1 minute of hitting 10min mark
- [ ] False positive rate <1%
- [ ] Works across all 16 tools' repositories
- [ ] Clear logging of detection and trigger events
- [ ] Exit codes: 0=OK, 1=throttled, 3=dependency/auth, 8=network

## Dependencies
- gh CLI (authenticated) OR GITHUB_TOKEN
- jq for JSON parsing
- date command with GNU extensions (or macOS-compatible fallback)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:07:15.526804311Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T17:50:08.469458787Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:07:15.526804311Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.1.1","title":"Implement dsr check command - detect throttled runs","description":"# Implement `dsr check` — Detect Throttled GH Actions Runs

## Purpose
Detect when GitHub Actions is throttled (runs stuck in queue) and provide structured data for automation.

## Data Source (GitHub REST API)
Use workflow runs JSON from `repos/{owner}/{repo}/actions/runs` (via `gh api` or curl fallback). Avoid parsing human output.

## Command Interface
```bash
dsr check <repo>
dsr check --all
 dsr check <repo> --threshold 10
 dsr check <repo> --json
```

## Detection Logic (robust)
1. Resolve short repo → full `owner/repo`
2. Fetch latest workflow runs via `gh_api` wrapper (with caching)
3. **Queued throttling**:
   - `status == "queued"` and `now - created_at > threshold`
4. **Stuck in_progress**:
   - `status == "in_progress"` and (`run_started_at == null` OR `now - run_started_at > threshold`)
5. Include `event`, `workflow_id`, `head_sha`, `html_url` for context

## Output (JSON)
```json
{
  "repo": "Dicklesworthstone/ntm",
  "status": "throttled",
  "oldest_queued_min": 15,
  "threshold_min": 10,
  "runs": [
    {"id":12345,"status":"queued","created_at":"...","workflow_id":999}
  ]
}
```

## Error Handling
- Auth missing → exit 3 (dependency/auth)
- Rate limit/network → exit 8 (network)
- Invalid args → exit 4

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Correctly flags queued/stuck runs using API timestamps
- [ ] JSON output includes run IDs + workflow IDs + html_url
- [ ] Uses cached responses (short TTL)
- [ ] Works across all configured repos","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:07:35.923073198Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T17:54:26.983516350Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T17:54:26.983498096Z","close_reason":"Implemented dsr check command with throttle detection. Works with JSON mode. ShellCheck clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:07:35.923073198Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:00.946605637Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:11.895264009Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:38:52.255711563Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:51:48.449588674Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-1jt.1.2","title":"Implement dsr watch command - continuous monitoring daemon","description":"# Implement `dsr watch` — Continuous Monitoring Daemon

## Purpose
Continuously monitor all configured repos and trigger fallback only when throttling is real, stable, and deduped.

## Design Requirements
- **Jittered polling** to avoid thundering herds
- **Debounce** repeated throttling signals (avoid flapping)
- **Deduplicate** by run ID so each stuck run triggers at most once
- **Resilient** to temporary API failures (backoff + retry)

## Command Interface
```bash
dsr watch
 dsr watch --daemon
 dsr watch --interval 60
 dsr watch --dry-run
 dsr watch --once
```

## Implementation Notes
- Reuse `dsr check --json`
- Track triggered run IDs in `~/.local/state/dsr/triggered.json`
- Use a lightweight lockfile to avoid multiple watcher instances
- On startup, run `dsr doctor --quick`
- Handle SIGTERM/SIGINT to persist state + release lock

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Polls all repos at configured interval
- [ ] Triggers fallback once per throttled run
- [ ] Exponential backoff on API errors
- [ ] Daemon mode survives restarts and logs state
- [ ] --once performs a single check + exit","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:07:49.072822562Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T17:52:36.467264866Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:07:49.072822562Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:15:20.660721260Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:01.213534509Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:12.167502295Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:38:52.515880942Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:51:52.647890515Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

## Purpose
Continuously monitor all configured repos and trigger fallback only when throttling is real, stable, and deduped.

## Design Requirements
- **Jittered polling** to avoid thundering herds
- **Debounce** repeated throttling signals (avoid flapping)
- **Deduplicate** by run ID so each stuck run triggers at most once
- **Resilient** to temporary API failures (backoff + retry)

## Command Interface
```bash
dsr watch
 dsr watch --daemon
 dsr watch --interval 60
 dsr watch --dry-run
 dsr watch --once
```

## Implementation Notes
- Reuse `dsr check --json`
- Track triggered run IDs in `~/.local/state/dsr/triggered.json`
- Use a lightweight lockfile to avoid multiple watcher instances
- On startup, run `dsr doctor --quick`
- Handle SIGTERM/SIGINT to persist state + release lock

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Triggers fallback once per throttled run
- [ ] Exponential backoff on API errors
- [ ] Daemon mode survives restarts and logs state

 {"id":"bd-1jt.1.3","title":"Implement dsr repos command - manage tool registry","description":"# Implement dsr repos command — manage tool registry

## Purpose
Manage the repo registry used by build/release/install. This is the authoritative source for targets, commands, and workflow mappings.

## Registry Layout (XDG)
`~/.config/dsr/repos.d/<tool>.yaml` (one file per tool)
- `schema_version: "1.0.0"`
- `repo`, `local_path`, `language`, `build_cmd`, `binary_name`
- `targets`, `workflow`, `act_job_map`, `checks`
- `artifact_naming`, `archive_format`, `enabled`

## Command Interface
```bash
dsr repos list
 dsr repos add <tool>
 dsr repos remove <tool>
 dsr repos info <tool> [--json]
 dsr repos validate
 dsr repos discover [--apply]
```

## Behavior
- Reads/writes `repos.d/` (merge all tool files deterministically)
- Validates required fields (repo, build_cmd, targets, workflow jobs)
- `discover` scans `/data/projects` for Cargo.toml/go.mod/package.json
- Discovery is **read-only** unless `--apply`
- Never creates new directories; discovery is **read-only** unless `--apply`
- Never uses global `cd` for git ops; use `git -C <path>`
- Uses `yq` when available for safe edits; otherwise read-only fallback

## Best-Practice Notes
- Normalize paths (absolute or `~`), reject relative
- Use guardrails for any file deletion (`repos remove`)
- stdout for JSON/paths, stderr for human output only

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] List shows all tools with language + targets
- [ ] Add/remove works and persists per-tool files
- [ ] Info shows complete build config
- [ ] Validation catches missing fields
- [ ] Discover finds repos without mutating config unless confirmed
- [ ] Registry uses `repos.d` (no monolithic `repos.yaml`)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:08:02.877692893Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:07:51.168912408Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T18:06:16.702769732Z","close_reason":"Implemented dsr repos command with list, add, remove, info, validate, discover, and sync subcommands. All ShellCheck warnings addressed. JSON schema validated.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:08:02.877692893Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:01.477484654Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:38:52.755481675Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:39:50.134696217Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-8cg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:50:47.636700054Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.1.4","title":"Implement dsr fallback command - trigger local build pipeline","description":"# Implement `dsr fallback` — Orchestrated Build + Release

## Purpose
End-to-end fallback pipeline when GH Actions is throttled: build, verify, release, and report.

## Command Interface
```bash
dsr fallback <tool>
 dsr fallback <tool> --version v1.2.3
 dsr fallback <tool> --build-only
 dsr fallback <tool> --skip-checks
 dsr fallback <tool> --dry-run
 dsr fallback <tool> --resume
```

## Flow (must be deterministic)
1. Resolve repo config + tag/commit (build from tag)
2. Run pre-release checks (unless --skip-checks)
3. Acquire lock + create run_id
4. Build all targets (act + native hosts)
5. Generate manifest + checksums + signatures
6. Verify artifacts
7. Create release and upload assets
8. Emit JSON summary + status report

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Works end-to-end without manual intervention
- [ ] Uses manifest for release validation
- [ ] Supports resume after partial failure
- [ ] Clear logs + JSON output (schema-compliant)
- [ ] --dry-run outputs a deterministic plan without side  {"id":"bd-1jt.1.5","title":"Create default repos.d registry with all 16 tools configured","description":"# Create Default repos.d Registry with All 16 Tools Configured

## Purpose
Ship a complete, ready-to-use repository registry so `dsr` can build/release/install the full Dicklesworthstone toolchain without manual setup.

## Why this matters
Most commands (`check`, `build`, `release`, installers) rely on accurate repo metadata. Missing or inconsistent entries cause brittle behavior and user confusion.

## Location
`~/.config/dsr/repos.d/` (created by `dsr config init`)
- One file per tool: `repos.d/<tool>.yaml`
- Each file contains `schema_version` + tool fields

## Required Fields (per tool)
- `repo` (owner/name)
- `local_path` (absolute path, usually under `/data/projects`, but allow overrides)
- `language` (rust|go|bun|node)
- `binary_name`
- `build_cmd` (reproducible, non-interactive)
- `targets` (os/arch list)
- `checks` (lint/test/typecheck commands)
- `workflow` + `act_job_map`
- `artifact_naming` + `archive_format`
- `enabled` (bool) to allow opt-out

## Guardrails
- Never create new directories under `/data/projects`
- Missing `local_path` → warn, do not mutate
- Use `yq` for safe writes when available; read-only fallback otherwise

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 16 tools configured with full metadata
- [ ] Paths validated (existence check + absolute)
- [ ] Workflows + job mappings verified
- [ ] Artifact naming matches installer expectations
- [ ] Registry lives in `repos.d/` (no monolithic `repos.yaml`)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:14:13.167130536Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:08:03.116054165Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:34:33.023057790Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-1jt.2.10","title":"Validate GoReleaser config compatibility for each tool","description":"# GoReleaser Config Validation

## Purpose
Ensure dsr can replicate GoReleaser outputs (targets, archives, naming) so fallback builds match official releases.

## Approach
- Prefer `goreleaser release --snapshot --skip-publish` when available
- Fallback: parse .goreleaser.yaml via yq
- Compare produced artifacts against expected naming template

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Targets and archive formats match GoReleaser config
- [ ] Naming templates produce identical asset names
- [ ] Fallback parsing works when goreleaser not installed","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:30:21.603418246Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T15:57:48.748847712Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.10","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:30:21.603418246Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.10","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:30:49.305063723Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.2.11","title":"Implement build workspace isolation + lock/state machine","description":"# Implement Build Workspace Isolation + Lock/State Machine

## Purpose
Prevent overlapping builds and enable reliable resume/retry by persisting build state per tool/version.

## Key Behaviors
- Acquire lock per tool+version (state dir)
- Create unique run_id + workspace (no overwrites)
- Persist state JSON with per-host status + artifacts
- Resume uses state to skip completed hosts

## Requirements
- Explicit error handling (no `set -e`)
- State updated after every host step
- Locks released on clean exit or timeout
- Stale lock detection (PID + timestamp + TTL)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Concurrent runs are blocked or queued deterministically
- [ ] State file created and updated per host step
- [ ] --resume continues from last known state
- [ ] Logs reference run_id + state path
- [ ] Lock files are atomic and auto-cleaned when stale","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:36:39.000455353Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T17:40:21.024490854Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T17:40:21.024473411Z","close_reason":"Implemented build workspace isolation with lock/state machine in src/build_state.sh. 16 tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.11","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:36:39.000455353Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.2.12","title":"Define artifact naming + build manifest schema","description":"# Define Artifact Naming + Build Manifest Schema

## Purpose
Standardize artifact names and produce a manifest JSON that release/install/tests can trust.

## Naming Contract
- `{tool}-{version}-{os}-{arch}[.exe]`
- `checksums.sha256` includes all binaries
- Optional `.minisig` for each artifact

## Manifest Schema (minimum)
- `tool`, `version`, `run_id`, `git_sha`, `schema_version`
- `artifacts[]`: name, target, sha256, size_bytes, signature_path
- `hosts[]`: host, status, duration_ms, log_path, retry_count
- `build_env`: toolchain versions, workflow/job metadata, act version

## Placement
- `~/.local/state/dsr/manifests/<tool>/<version>/<run_id>.json`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Manifest produced for every build
- [ ] Release + installer use manifest as source of truth
- [ ] Tests validate manifest schema
- [ ] Manifest includes toolchain + host metadata for auditability","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:36:45.903938160Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T15:54:50.125203319Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T15:49:35.088175561Z","close_reason":"Created schemas/manifest.json (JSON Schema for build manifests), docs/ARTIFACT_NAMING.md (comprehensive naming convention docs), and scripts/tests/fixtures/manifest-example.json (test fixture). Defines {tool}-{version}-{os}-{arch} naming, manifest structure with artifacts, hosts, signatures, and SBOM references.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.12","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:36:45.903938160Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

## Deliverables

- Known exceptions (jobs not runnable in act)
- Per-host overrides (linux/amd64 vs linux/arm64)
- `repos.d/<tool>.yaml` `act_job_map` entries for each tool

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Each tool has an act-compatible mapping
- [ ] Unsupported jobs documented and skipped
- [ ] Artifact paths consistent across tools
- [ ] Overrides documented for non-standard workflows","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:36:52.346436490Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:08:18.214248069Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.13","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:36:52.346436490Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.13","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:31.371618554Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-1jt.2.14","title":"Build from tag/commit for reproducibility","description":"# Build From Tag/Commit for Reproducibility

## Purpose
Ensure fallback builds are reproducible by building from the exact tag/commit that will be released.

## Behavior
- Resolve version → tag → commit SHA
- Support explicit `--ref <sha|tag>` override
- Use fresh workspace (no dirty tree unless --allow-dirty)
- Record git SHA in manifest + logs
- Use `git -C <repo>` for all git operations

## Git Plumbing (No Status Parsing)
- Dirty check: `git diff-index --quiet HEAD --` (exit code)
- Tag existence: `git show-ref --tags --verify refs/tags/<tag>`
- Resolve SHA: `git rev-parse <ref>`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Builds use tagged commit, not local HEAD
- [ ] Dirty working trees produce clear errors
- [ ] Manifest contains git SHA + resolved ref
- [ ] `--ref` supports both tags and full SHAs","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:27:10.707996895Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T17:35:52.120988167Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.14","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:27:52.253058133Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.14","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:27:54.701273926Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.14","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:27:10.707996895Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.14","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:27:56.742094781Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.14","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:27:59.320434061Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.2.15","title":"Host selection + concurrency limits per host","description":"# Host Selection + Concurrency Limits Per Host

## Purpose
Choose the best host for each target based on health and enforce per-host concurrency to avoid overload.

## Behavior
- Select host using health + capability matrix from `hosts.yaml`
- Allow overrides: `--host`, `--max-parallel`, per-host concurrency caps
- Queue builds when a host is at capacity (FIFO with run_id ordering)
- Emit JSON details for scheduling decisions

## Design Notes
- Deterministic selection: same inputs → same host choice
- Never exceed per-host concurrency limits
- Record queue decisions in state/logs for postmortems

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Host selection deterministic and configurable
- [ ] Per-host concurrency limits enforced
- [ ] Queue/defer decisions logged with run_id + target
- [ ] JSON output includes host selection rationale","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:27:17.923979053Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T15:53:04.514928295Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.15","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:28:08.198973905Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.15","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:27:17.923979053Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.15","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:28:15.521885678Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.15","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:28:13.167796475Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.2.16","title":"Artifact retention + prune command (disk pressure guard)","description":"# Artifact Retention + Prune Command (Disk Pressure Guard)

## Purpose
Prevent disk exhaustion by pruning old build artifacts and cached downloads safely.

## Behavior
- `dsr prune --dry-run` shows what would be removed
- Retention policy configurable (keep last N runs per tool)
- Require explicit confirmation unless `--yes`
- Log every pruned path to state logs
- Hard safety: refuse to delete outside `DSR_STATE_DIR` and `DSR_CACHE_DIR`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Safe dry-run output
- [ ] Retention policy configurable
- [ ] Prune logs record what was removed
- [ ] Guardrails prevent accidental deletion of non-dsr paths","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:27:23.648918021Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:10:13.480918878Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.16","depends_on_id":"bd-11r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:09:46.986203142Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.16","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:27:23.648918021Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.16","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:28:23.490018340Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.16","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.5.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:28:26.407796120Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.16","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:39:41.648734026Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.16","depends_on_id":"bd-8cg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:50:46.778018160Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
